task.name: Stroop Color-Word Task
reference_literature: Stroop, J.R. (1935). Studies of interference in serial verbal reactions. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 18(6), 643-662.
MacLeod, C.M. (1991). Half a century of research on the Stroop effect: An integrative review. Psychological Bulletin, 109(2), 163-203.

Spieler, D.H., Balota, D.A., & Faust, M.E. (1996). Stroop performance in healthy younger and older adults and in individuals with dementia of the Alzheimer's type. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance, 22(2), 461-479.
Crump, M.J.C., McDonnell, J.V., & Gureckis, T.M. (2013). Evaluating Amazon's Mechanical Turk as a tool for experimental behavioral research. PLoS ONE, 8(3), e57410.
Hilbig, B.E. (2016). Reaction time effects in lab- versus web-based research: Experimental evidence. Behavior Research Methods, 48, 1718-1724.

stimuli.description: Color word displayed in matching ink color (congruent) — respond with first letter of ink color

detection.selector: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if(!el) return false; var p = el.querySelector('p'); if(!p) return false; var text = p.textContent.trim().toLowerCase(); var style = p.getAttribute('style'); if(!style) return false; var cm = style.match(/color:\s*(red|green|blue|yellow)/); if(!cm) return false; var color = cm[1].toLowerCase(); return (text === 'red' || text === 'green' || text === 'blue' || text === 'yellow') && text === color; })()

response.response_key_js: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if(!el) return null; var p = el.querySelector('p'); if(!p) return null; var style = p.getAttribute('style'); if(!style) return null; var cm = style.match(/color:\s*(red|green|blue|yellow)/); if(!cm) return null; return cm[1].charAt(0); })()

stimuli.description: Color word displayed in different ink color (incongruent) — respond with first letter of ink color

detection.selector: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if(!el) return false; var p = el.querySelector('p'); if(!p) return false; var text = p.textContent.trim().toLowerCase(); var style = p.getAttribute('style'); if(!style) return false; var cm = style.match(/color:\s*(red|green|blue|yellow)/); if(!cm) return false; var color = cm[1].toLowerCase(); return (text === 'red' || text === 'green' || text === 'blue' || text === 'yellow') && (color === 'red' || color === 'green' || color === 'blue' || color === 'yellow') && text !== color; })()

response.response_key_js: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if(!el) return null; var p = el.querySelector('p'); if(!p) return null; var style = p.getAttribute('style'); if(!style) return null; var cm = style.match(/color:\s*(red|green|blue|yellow)/); if(!cm) return null; return cm[1].charAt(0); })()

task_specific.response_mapping: first letter of ink color (r=red, g=green, b=blue, y=yellow)

autocorrelation.rationale: Luce (1986) and Gilden (2001) document modest lag-1 autocorrelation (r ~ 0.1-0.2) in choice RT tasks. The short task (30 trials) limits the detectability of this effect, but it is a fundamental property of human RT series.

fatigue_drift.rationale: With only 30 trials, fatigue drift is minimal but present. Buck (1966) and Welford (1980) report gradual slowing over short sessions. A conservative 1 ms/trial yields ~30 ms total drift.

post_error_slowing.rationale: Rabbitt (1966) first documented post-error slowing. Dutilh et al. (2012) found ~50-80 ms PES in Stroop-type conflict tasks. Online settings may show slightly larger PES due to more cautious post-error adjustment.

condition_repetition.rationale: Gratton et al. (1992) demonstrated congruency sequence effects (CSE) in the Stroop task. Condition repetition facilitation is typically 10-25 ms. The asymmetry reflects greater repetition priming benefit than alternation cost.

pink_noise.rationale: Gilden (1997, 2001) demonstrated 1/f noise structure in human RT series with Hurst exponents around 0.7-0.8. SD is kept moderate given the short task length of 30 trials.

post_interrupt_slowing.rationale: No trial interrupt / stop-signal component in this Stroop task.

between_subject_jitter.rationale: Online Stroop studies (Crump et al., 2013; Hilbig, 2016) show substantial between-subject variability in mean RT (SD ~60-100ms). The congruency effect magnitude also varies across individuals (SD ~15ms). Sigma/tau scaling reflects individual differences in RT distribution shape. Accuracy varies modestly across participants in this easy task.

pilot.rationale: The experiment has 30 trials total (15 congruent + 15 incongruent) in a single block. With equal condition probability, 6 trials should reliably sample both conditions. There are no practice trials or multiple blocks.

phase_detection.test: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if(!el) return false; var p = el.querySelector('p'); if(!p) return false; var style = p.getAttribute('style') || ''; return /color:\s*(red|green|blue|yellow)/.test(style); })()
phase_detection.instructions: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('.jspsych-instructions-nav') || document.querySelector('#jspsych-instructions-back') || document.querySelector('#jspsych-instructions-next'); if(el) return true; var content = document.querySelector('.jspsych-content'); if(!content) return false; var text = content.textContent || ''; return (text.indexOf('Welcome') !== -1 || text.indexOf('experiment') !== -1 || text.indexOf('Press Space') !== -1) && !document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus p[style*="color"]'); })()
phase_detection.feedback: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if(!el) return false; var text = el.textContent || ''; return text.indexOf('Correct') !== -1 || text.indexOf('Incorrect') !== -1 || text.indexOf('Too slow') !== -1; })()

timing.response_window_js: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if(!el) return false; var p = el.querySelector('p'); if(!p) return false; var style = p.getAttribute('style') || ''; return /color:\s*(red|green|blue|yellow)/.test(style); })()
timing.trial_context_js: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if(!el) return ''; var p = el.querySelector('p'); if(!p) return ''; var text = p.textContent.trim().toLowerCase(); var style = p.getAttribute('style') || ''; var cm = style.match(/color:\s*(\w+)/); var color = cm ? cm[1] : '?'; return 'word=' + text + ',color=' + color; })()
